reasoning: Lesotho generally experiences very minimal conflict, with the ACLED baseline average of 'strategic developments' essentially at 0 over the 360 days leading up to October 2025. To cross the threshold required by the question (10-fold increase over the baseline of 1), Lesotho would need to experience 11 or more 'strategic developments' (such as large-scale arrests, military movements, or political violence) within the referenced 30-day windows in October, November, or December 2025/January 2026. A thorough search of news and crisis databases covering late 2025 and early 2026 shows no evidence of such events occurring within Lesotho. In fact, South African Development Community (SADC) leaders specifically removed Lesotho from their crisis agenda around early 2026 citing "positive progress in reforms and stability." Aside from isolated items like the arrest of two soldiers selling arms or Lesotho's PM holding meetings in South Africa, there was no major unrest or wave of ACLED-codable non-violent tactical/strategic maneuvers. Therefore, it is virtually certain that the count did not reach 11+ in any of the stipulated 30-day periods. With all resolution dates now in the past, I assign a 2% baseline probability due to the theoretical minor risk of sudden retroactive ACLED data dumps.
